steps.title: Identify what the example must show
steps.content: The text says most countries had annual increases in international tourism revenue in 2017 and 2018, but the trend "wasn’t universal." The blank must complete an example of a country that did not follow that upward trend — specifically one that saw a decrease in revenue.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — in each year shown, the revenue from tourism by residents of those countries was greater than the revenue from international tourism.: the table contains no data on domestic tourism revenue, so this claim cannot be supported. ✗
  • Choice B — Japan's international tourism revenue was higher in 2016 than it was in 2018.: factually wrong — Japan’s revenue grew from \$33.5B in 2016 to \$45.3B in 2018. ✗
  • Choice C — South Korea's annual international tourism revenue decreased from 2016 to 2017.: South Korea’s revenue fell from \$21.0B in 2016 to \$17.2B in 2017, directly illustrating a country that broke the upward trend. ✓
  • Choice D — in each year shown, Thailand had higher international tourism income than any of the other countries.: Thailand’s consistent lead has no bearing on whether all countries saw increases — it does not show any break from the trend. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
